articles.title: Movie Magic: The Secret Helpers in Films
articles.summary: A new movie called Wuthering Heights, based on an old book, has people talking. In the film, there is a scene showing a character's back with scars. Many viewers thought it was the main actor, Jacob Elordi, but it might have been someone else. The movie used body doubles, who are helpers that stand in for actors. One body double named Bo Ponomari shared his story. He is shorter than Jacob but looked similar enough to help. Bo had fake scars put on his back to match Jacob's. His job was to help test cameras and lighting when Jacob was busy. Sometimes, movies mix scenes with actors and body doubles, so viewers might not know who they are seeing. Body doubles are important but often unseen heroes in making movies.

articles.title: What the Spring Statement Tells Us About Money in the UK
articles.summary: The Spring Statement is a speech by the Chancellor, Rachel Reeves, who talks about the UK's money plans. She will share new guesses about how much the economy will grow, how prices might change, and how many people might not have jobs. These guesses come from a group called the Office for Budget Responsibility (OBR), which checks the government's spending. The Spring Statement helps the government decide if they should spend more or less money or change taxes. This year, the statement won't say if the government is following its money rules, but it will include updates like easier taxes for farms and more help for schools with special needs. Even though oil prices have gone up because of strikes on Iran, these guesses don't include that. People are still watching to see if borrowing and inflation might be lower, but growth and jobs could be worse.

articles.title: Music Festival Noise Plans Worry People
articles.summary: People living and working near a big music festival in Cornwall are worried about plans to make the music louder. The festival, called Boardmasters, happens in Newquay for five days in August. Organizers want to increase the noise levels and play music later at night. They say this will help the festival stay popular and fun. But hotel owners, local leaders, and residents say louder music will disturb people and businesses. They think the festival should plan better instead of making more noise. A council will decide on the plans soon.

articles.title: Drones Hit Amazon's Computer Buildings in UAE and Bahrain
articles.summary: Amazon's cloud computing business, called AWS, says drones damaged three of its buildings in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) and Bahrain. This happened after the US and Israel attacked Iran over the weekend. On Sunday, objects hit a data center in the UAE, causing sparks and fire. In Bahrain, a drone strike near a facility caused power and connection problems. Amazon confirmed on Monday that drone strikes caused these outages. The drones broke parts of the buildings, stopped power, and even caused water damage from firefighting. Amazon is working to fix the damage and restore services, but it might take time. They advise customers in the region to back up their data and move their work to other AWS facilities around the world. This shows how important technology buildings can be at risk during wars.

articles.title: Young Workers Share Their Money Stories
articles.summary: This article talks about how young people in their 20s and 30s are doing with money. It shares stories from two workers. Andrew is a 24-year-old bartender in Guildford. He works long hours but his shifts can change suddenly, making it hard to plan. His rent went up, and he sometimes uses apps to get his pay early. He saved some money last year but had to spend it when work was slow. Andrew feels stressed and has given up on working in restaurants. He now wants to go to university to find a better job. Jack is a 24-year-old technical operator in Salford. He bought a house with his girlfriend because interest rates went down, making it cheaper to borrow money. He lived at home first to save money from his salary. The article shows that money can be tough for young workers, but some find ways to get ahead.

articles.title: Cillian Murphy and Stars at Peaky Blinders Movie Premiere
articles.summary: Cillian Murphy and other stars went to a big movie premiere in Birmingham for the Peaky Blinders film called The Immortal Man. Many fans dressed in flat caps and tweed came to see the actors like Rebecca Ferguson and Tim Roth. The film continues the TV show about Tommy Shelby, a gang leader, during World War Two. Steven Knight, who created the show, said Birmingham is a star of the film because it was filmed there. Murphy agreed that the city and Peaky Blinders are connected. The premiere was held in Birmingham to honor the fans and the show's history. The film will be in cinemas for two weeks and then on Netflix. Fans from places like Derby came to meet the stars, showing how much they love the show.
